patterns.name: Input / Output
patterns.notes: Learn standard I/O methods: cin/cout in C++, Scanner/System.out in Java, input()/print() in Python.

patterns.name: Time Complexity
patterns.notes: Big-O analysis: O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), O(n^2), O(2^n), O(n!). Analyze loops, recursion, and divide-and-conquer.

patterns.name: Space Complexity
patterns.notes: Memory usage analysis: auxiliary space vs input space. O(1) in-place algorithms vs O(n) extra space.

patterns.name: Dry Run
patterns.notes: Manually trace through code with sample inputs. Essential for debugging and understanding algorithm flow.

patterns.name: Traversal
patterns.notes: Single pass, two-pass, reverse traversal. Basic building block for all array problems.

questions.name: Find Numbers with Even Number of Digits

patterns.name: Prefix Sum
patterns.notes: Build prefix[i] = prefix[i-1] + arr[i]. Use for O(1) range sum queries: sum(l,r) = prefix[r] - prefix[l-1].

patterns.name: Suffix Sum
patterns.notes: Build suffix[i] = suffix[i+1] + arr[i]. Used for right-side cumulative computations.

patterns.name: Sliding Window
patterns.notes: Fixed or variable window. Maintain left/right pointers. Expand right, shrink left when condition breaks. O(n).

patterns.name: Two Pointer
patterns.notes: Two pointers moving towards each other or in same direction. Great for sorted arrays, pair finding.

questions.name: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted

patterns.name: Kadane
patterns.notes: Maximum subarray sum. Keep current sum and reset to 0 if negative. maxEnding = max(arr[i], maxEnding + arr[i]).

patterns.name: Dutch National Flag
patterns.notes: 3-way partitioning. Sort array of 0s, 1s, 2s. Three pointers: low, mid, high. O(n), O(1) space.

patterns.name: Merge Intervals
patterns.notes: Sort by start time, then merge overlapping intervals. Common in scheduling/calendar problems.

patterns.name: Cyclic Sort
patterns.notes: Place each element at its correct index. For arrays with values 1..n. O(n) time, O(1) space.

questions.name: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array

patterns.name: Difference Array
patterns.notes: diff[i] = arr[i] - arr[i-1]. Range update: diff[l] += val, diff[r+1] -= val. Prefix sum to restore.

patterns.name: Sum
patterns.notes: Pair sum, triplet sum, four sum. Use hashing or two-pointer after sorting.

patterns.name: Max/Min
patterns.notes: Find max/min in array, second max, kth max. Use linear scan or heap for kth.

questions.name: Kth Largest Element in an Array

patterns.name: Subarray
patterns.notes: Contiguous subarray problems. Use prefix sum, sliding window, or DP depending on constraints.

patterns.name: Rotate
patterns.notes: Array rotation. Reverse whole, reverse first k, reverse remaining n-k. O(n), O(1).

patterns.name: Missing Number
patterns.notes: Find missing number in 1..n. XOR all indices and values, or use sum formula n*(n+1)/2.

patterns.name: Frequency Count
patterns.notes: Count character frequencies using array[26] or hashmap. Key pattern for anagram/permutation problems.

questions.name: First Unique Character in a String

patterns.name: Sliding Window
patterns.notes: Variable/fixed window on strings. Expand right, shrink left when condition breaks.

patterns.name: Two Pointer
patterns.notes: Left and right pointers on string. Used for palindrome checking, reversing, comparing from ends.

patterns.name: Hashing
patterns.notes: Use hashmap/set for O(n) string lookups. Pattern matching, duplicate detection, group anagrams.

patterns.name: Palindrome
patterns.notes: Two pointers from center expanding outwards. Check odd/even length palindromes. Manacher for O(n).

patterns.name: KMP
patterns.notes: Knuth-Morris-Pratt. Compute LPS (Longest Prefix Suffix) array. O(n+m) pattern matching.

questions.name: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

patterns.name: Rabin Karp
patterns.notes: Rolling hash for pattern matching. Use modulo arithmetic to compare hashes in O(1). Handle collisions.

questions.name: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

patterns.name: Z Algorithm
patterns.notes: Z-array where Z[i] = longest substring starting at i matching prefix. O(n) for pattern matching.

questions.name: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String

patterns.name: Pick / Not Pick
patterns.notes: Fundamental recursion: at each step, decide to include or exclude current element. Generates all subsets.

patterns.name: Backtracking
patterns.notes: Try all possibilities, undo (backtrack) when dead end. Decision tree with pruning.

patterns.name: Divide & Conquer
patterns.notes: Divide problem into subproblems, solve recursively, combine results. e.g., Merge Sort, Quick Sort.

patterns.name: Generate All
patterns.notes: Generate all possible combinations/permutations/arrangements. Use recursion + backtracking.

questions.name: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number

patterns.name: Subsequence
patterns.notes: A subsequence maintains order but need not be contiguous. Pick/not-pick for each element.

patterns.name: Permutation
patterns.notes: Arrange elements in different orders. Swap-based recursion or used[] boolean array.

patterns.name: Combination
patterns.notes: Choose k elements from n. Recursion with start index to avoid duplicates.

patterns.name: Bubble
patterns.notes: Repeatedly swap adjacent if out of order. O(n^2). Optimize with early termination if no swaps.

patterns.name: Selection
patterns.notes: Find minimum and place at beginning. O(n^2). Makes O(n) swaps (useful when write cost is high).

patterns.name: Insertion
patterns.notes: Build sorted array one element at a time. Insert current into correct position in sorted part. O(n^2). Best for nearly sorted.

patterns.name: Merge
patterns.notes: Divide array, sort halves, merge. O(n log n), O(n) space. Stable sort. Used in external sorting.

questions.name: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self

patterns.name: Quick
patterns.notes: Pick pivot, partition, recurse. O(n log n) average, O(n^2) worst. QuickSelect for kth element.

questions.name: Kth Largest Element in an Array

patterns.name: Heap
patterns.notes: Build max/min heap, extract root repeatedly. O(n log n). Priority queue implementation.

patterns.name: Counting
patterns.notes: Count frequencies, then rebuild array. O(n+k). Only for small range of integer values.

patterns.name: Radix
patterns.notes: Sort digit by digit (LSD or MSD). O(d*(n+b)) where d=digit count, b=base. Great for integers/strings.

patterns.name: Classic Binary Search
patterns.notes: Search sorted array. low=0, high=n-1, mid=low+(high-low)/2. O(log n).

patterns.name: First/Last Occurrence
patterns.notes: Find leftmost/rightmost target. Modify comparison to not stop at first match. low < high vs low <= high.

questions.name: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array

patterns.name: Search on Answer
patterns.notes: Binary search on numeric answer range, not on array. Check if mid satisfies condition, then narrow range.

patterns.name: Binary Search on Array
patterns.notes: Search in 2D matrix, search space optimization. Reduce search space by half each iteration.

questions.name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array

patterns.name: Peak Element
patterns.notes: Find element that is greater than neighbors. Binary search on unsorted array using slope direction.

questions.name: Peak Index in a Mountain Array

patterns.name: Rotated Array
patterns.notes: Find pivot (min element) using binary search. Compare mid with high to determine sorted half.

questions.name: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array

patterns.name: Fast Slow Pointer
patterns.notes: Slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2. Find middle, detect cycle, find start of cycle, find kth from end.

questions.name: Remove Nth Node From End of List

patterns.name: Reverse
patterns.notes: Iterative: prev, curr, next pointers. Recursive: reverse rest, point next->next to curr.

patterns.name: Merge
patterns.notes: Merge two sorted lists. Dummy node technique. Compare heads and attach smaller.

patterns.name: Cycle Detection
patterns.notes: Floyd's algorithm. Fast/slow pointer. If they meet, cycle exists. Reset one to head, step both same speed to find cycle start.

patterns.name: Dummy Node
patterns.notes: Create dummy node pointing to head. Simplifies edge cases (deleting head, empty list).

patterns.name: Monotonic Stack
patterns.notes: Stack with increasing or decreasing order. Push while maintaining order, pop when condition breaks.

patterns.name: Next Greater
patterns.notes: Find next greater element to right. Traverse from right, maintain decreasing stack. NGE on left = traverse from left.

patterns.name: Previous Smaller
patterns.notes: Find previous smaller element. Traverse from left, maintain increasing stack.

patterns.name: Expression
patterns.notes: Postfix/infix/prefix evaluation. Use stacks for operands and operators. Shunting-yard algorithm.

patterns.name: Histogram
patterns.notes: Largest rectangle in histogram. Monotonic stack with indices. Area = height[popped] * (curr - stack[-1] - 1).

patterns.name: Min Stack
patterns.notes: Design stack with push/pop/top/getMin in O(1). Maintain auxiliary stack or store pairs (val, minSoFar).

patterns.name: Sliding Window Maximum
patterns.notes: Monotonic deque. Maintain decreasing deque of indices. Remove out-of-window indices. front is max.

patterns.name: Circular Queue
patterns.notes: Fixed-size circular buffer. front and rear pointers wrap around using modulo. Handle full/empty states.

patterns.name: BFS Queue
patterns.notes: Standard queue for level-order traversal. Used in BFS on trees and graphs.

patterns.name: Frequency Map
patterns.notes: Count frequencies using hashmap. Key pattern for duplicates, anagrams, majority element.

patterns.name: Pair Sum
patterns.notes: Use hashset/hashmap to find pairs that sum to target. Check if target - current exists in map.

patterns.name: Prefix Hash
patterns.notes: Store prefix values in hashmap. Used for subarray sum, longest subarray with equal 0/1, etc.

questions.name: Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters

patterns.name: DFS
patterns.notes: Preorder, inorder, postorder. Recursive and iterative (using stack). Foundation for tree problems.

patterns.name: BFS
patterns.notes: Level order traversal using queue. Process level by level.

patterns.name: Traversal
patterns.notes: Special traversals: vertical order, top view, bottom view, boundary traversal, diagonal traversal.

patterns.name: Diameter
patterns.notes: Longest path between any two nodes (may not pass through root). Calculate height + update max diameter.

patterns.name: LCA
patterns.notes: Lowest Common Ancestor. Recursively find LCA. If both sides return non-null, current is LCA.

questions.name: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree

patterns.name: Path Sum
patterns.notes: Root-to-leaf sum, any path sum. Use DFS with target tracking. Subtract current val from target at each node.

patterns.name: Balanced Tree
patterns.notes: Check if tree is height-balanced (height diff <= 1 for all nodes). Post-order check with height propagation.

patterns.name: Insert
patterns.notes: Insert into BST. Traverse left or right based on value. Insert as leaf. Recursive or iterative.

questions.name: Insert into a Binary Search Tree

patterns.name: Delete
patterns.notes: 3 cases: leaf (just remove), one child (replace with child), two children (replace with inorder successor).

patterns.name: Search
patterns.notes: Compare root: if equal return, else go left/right. O(log n) average, O(n) worst for skewed.

questions.name: Search in a Binary Search Tree

patterns.name: Floor/Ceil
patterns.notes: Floor: largest value <= target. Ceil: smallest value >= target. Traverse BST tracking candidate.

patterns.name: Kth Smallest
patterns.notes: Inorder traversal gives sorted order. Use stack/recursion with counter. Morris traversal for O(1) space.

questions.name: Kth Smallest Element in a BST

patterns.name: Top K
patterns.notes: Min-heap of size K to keep K largest. For K smallest, use max-heap. O(n log k).

questions.name: Kth Largest Element in an Array

patterns.name: K Largest
patterns.notes: Min-heap of size K. For each element push, if size > K pop smallest. Heap contains K largest.

questions.name: Kth Largest Element in a Stream

patterns.name: Merge K
patterns.notes: Push all heads into min-heap. Pop min, push its next. O(n log k) for K sorted lists.

questions.name: Kth Smallest Element in a Sorted Matrix

patterns.name: Median
patterns.notes: Two heaps: max-heap for left half, min-heap for right half. Balance sizes. Median = peek of larger or avg.

patterns.name: BFS
patterns.notes: Queue-based level expansion. Shortest path in unweighted graph. O(V+E).

patterns.name: DFS
patterns.notes: Recursive/stack-based traversal. Used for connected components, topological sort, cycle detection.

patterns.name: Topological Sort
patterns.notes: Linear ordering of DAG. Kahn's algorithm (indegree + BFS) or DFS + stack. Used for dependency resolution.

patterns.name: Cycle Detection
patterns.notes: DFS with visited/visiting/done states. Union-Find for undirected graphs. Kahn's algorithm for DAG check.

patterns.name: Shortest Path
patterns.notes: BFS for unweighted. Dijkstra for positive weights. Bellman-Ford for negative weights (V-1 iterations).

patterns.name: Dijkstra
patterns.notes: Min-heap priority queue. dist[source]=0. While PQ not empty: pop min, relax neighbors. O((V+E) log V).

patterns.name: MST
patterns.notes: Prim's (grow from one node) or Kruskal's (sort edges, union-find). Minimum spanning tree of graph.

questions.name: Min Cost to Connect All Points

patterns.name: Union Find
patterns.notes: Disjoint Set Union. find() with path compression. union() by rank/size. Near O(alpha(n)) amortized.

patterns.name: Bipartite
patterns.notes: Check if graph can be colored with 2 colors. BFS/DFS with color array. Odd cycle = not bipartite.

patterns.name: Memoization
patterns.notes: Top-down DP. Recursion + cache (array/hashmap). Solve original problem, cache subproblem results.

patterns.name: Tabulation
patterns.notes: Bottom-up DP. Build table from base case to target. Iterative, avoids recursion overhead.

patterns.name: 1D DP
patterns.notes: Single array/state DP. House robber, fibonacci, climbing stairs. dp[i] depends on dp[i-1], dp[i-2].

patterns.name: 2D DP
patterns.notes: Matrix/grid DP. dp[i][j] depends on dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]. Path finding, edit distance.

patterns.name: Knapsack
patterns.notes: 0/1 Knapsack: pick each item once. Unbounded: pick unlimited. Subset sum, partition equal subset sum.

patterns.name: LIS
patterns.notes: Longest Increasing Subsequence. O(n^2) DP or O(n log n) patience sorting (binary search on tails array).

patterns.name: LCS
patterns.notes: Longest Common Subsequence. dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]+1 if match else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

patterns.name: Partition
patterns.notes: Partition array into subsets with given properties. Subset sum, equal partition, palindrome partitioning.

patterns.name: DP on Trees
patterns.notes: Tree DP. Post-order traversal, combine children results. House Robber III, diameter, max path sum.

patterns.name: DP on Graph
patterns.notes: DP on DAGs, shortest/longest paths in DAG using topological sort. TSP is classic hard DP on graph.

patterns.name: Interval
patterns.notes: Greedy on intervals. Sort by end time, pick non-overlapping. Classic: N meetings in one room.

questions.name: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons

patterns.name: Scheduling
patterns.notes: Job sequencing with deadlines, task scheduling. Sort by profit/deadline, use min-heap or sorted set.

questions.name: Maximum Number of Events That Can Be Attended

patterns.name: Sorting Based
patterns.notes: Sort input, then apply greedy choice. Fractional knapsack, activity selection, minimum platforms.

patterns.name: Insert
patterns.notes: Insert word into trie. Node has children[26] and isEnd flag. Create nodes as needed along path.

patterns.name: Search
patterns.notes: Traverse trie following characters. Check isEnd flag at the end. Return false if path breaks.

patterns.name: Prefix
patterns.notes: Check if prefix exists in trie. Traverse characters, return false if any missing. Don't check isEnd.

patterns.name: Range Query
patterns.notes: Segment tree for range queries (sum, min, max, gcd). Build tree O(n), query O(log n).

patterns.name: Point Update
patterns.notes: Update single element in segment tree. Walk down to leaf, update, propagate up. O(log n).

patterns.name: Lazy Propagation
patterns.notes: Range update (add value to range) with lazy tags. Defer updates until needed. O(log n) for range update/query.

questions.name: Range Sum Query with Range Updates

patterns.name: Min/Max/Sum Query
patterns.notes: Segment tree variants for min, max, gcd, xor queries. Merge operation changes based on requirement.
